title: Cleanup whether to enter user namespace

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
I am afraid we won't be able to rm files not owned by the rootless user without entering the user namespace.
Yes I just thought of the same thing.
This PR no longer fixes the issue, but I think it cleans up the code and adds

One question: can we still remove containers without entering the user namespace? We may need to delete files not owned by the current user.

I was thinking we needed a way to enter the userns without joining the pause process.

> I was thinking we needed a way to enter the userns without joining the pause process.

We have a fallback in place for that.  When the pause process cannot be joined in some cases it is enough to just create a new namespace.  Looks like something is going wrong there when not using systemd
